diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with epigastric pain, weakness, and pre-syncope, and was found to have atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular rate (RVR) noted on EKG. Additionally, a lower extremity ultrasound revealed an occlusive right greater saphenous vein thrombus, supporting the concern for DVT. However, subsequent evaluations ruled out the presence of DVT in both lower extremities.
